Responses

The OpenAQ API and Python SDK use a common response format to ensure consistency. The Python SDK automatically deserializes API responses and transforms raw JSON into convenient Python objects with dot notation access. This design simplifies data retrieval and reduces boilerplate code while maintaining flexibility. You can still access raw JSON or dictionary formats when needed. Understanding this response structure helps you work effectively with any OpenAQ resource.

Response structure

Section titled “Response structure”

All response objects follow a common top-level form, with specific data objects for each resource within the results. The results object in each response remains a list, or array in JSON. This applies even when requesting a single object by keys, like in a get() function call, or when filtering removes all possible results. In the latter case, you receive an empty list.

Find the structure of individual resource response results in their respective pages in Resources section of the docs.

Accessing response data

Section titled “Accessing response data”

The SDK provides three ways to access response data, depending on your needs.

Response objects

Section titled “Response objects”

By default, resource methods deserialize OpenAQ API JSON responses into data objects for easier access. These data objects support convenient dot notation access to attributes. You can retrieve values directly from the response structure shown above.

Dictionary

Section titled “Dictionary”

You can also return responses as a Python dictionary. All resource responses include a dict() method that allows property access using either Python dictionary key syntax:

or the built-in get():

Just like in the deserialized object, the results key has a Python list for its value.

JSON

Section titled “JSON”

Response objects also provide the option to return the raw JSON string of the response payload. You can access this on all response objects by using the json() method:
